question: Why is nginx restarting?
root_cause: Pod is scheduled on control-plane node, violating best practices for workload isolation
findings: Pod is scheduled on control-plane node (kubernetes-demo-cluster-control-plane)
recommendations: Add nodeSelector to pod specification to enforce scheduling on worker nodes
Apply taints to control-plane nodes and ensure pods have matching tolerations
Verify node resource availability and adjust kube-scheduler configurations if needed

question: Why is nginx restarting?
root_cause: Pod scheduled on control-plane node indicating suboptimal scheduling and potential single point of failure
findings: Pod is scheduled on control-plane node (kubernetes-demo-cluster-control-plane)
recommendations: Add taint to control-plane node to prevent regular Pods from scheduling there
Update Pod specification with nodeSelector to target worker nodes
Verify cluster node roles and ensure control-plane nodes have proper taints

question: Why is nginx restarting?
root_cause: Improper pod scheduling on control-plane node leading to potential resource contention
findings: Scheduled on control-plane node (kubernetes-demo-cluster-control-plane) which is not optimal for workloads
QoS Class is BestEffort which may indicate potential resource contention
recommendations: Update pod specification to use nodeSelector or taints to ensure scheduling only on dedicated worker nodes
Define resource requests/limits to upgrade QoS class from BestEffort to Burstable/Guaranteed

findings: Scheduled on control-plane node (kubernetes-demo-cluster-control-plane) which is not optimal for workloads

question: Why is nginx restarting?
root_cause: Improper pod scheduling on control-plane nodes with BestEffort QoS class leading to resource contention and abnormal CPU metrics
findings: Prometheus reports 0.0% CPU usage anomaly for workloads
Kubernetes pods scheduled on control-plane node (kubernetes-demo-cluster-control-plane) instead of worker nodes
Pod QoS Class is BestEffort indicating potential resource contention
recommendations: Reschedule pods to dedicated worker nodes using node selectors or taints
Update pod QoS class to Guaranteed or Burstable by allocating resource requests/limits
Verify Prometheus scraping configuration for node-exporter metrics
Implement Kubernetes node affinity rules for workload placement

question: Why is crash-demo restarting?
root_cause: Container exit code 1 triggers CrashLoopBackOff due to intentional test configuration
findings: Container command explicitly exits with error code 1
Pod remains in CrashLoopBackOff state with 5 restarts
Kubernetes' CrashLoopBackOff mechanism is actively preventing stable operation
recommendations: Modify container command to exit with non-fatal code (e.g. 0) for test scenarios
Implement proper error handling in test container logic
Verify test environment configuration aligns with expected behavior

question: Why is crash-demo in CrashLoopBackOff?
root_cause: DNS resolution failure preventing image pull from registry-1.docker.io
findings: kubernetes: DNS server at 172.18.0.1 is misbehaving, preventing resolution of registry-1.docker.io during image pull
loki: Pod is entering CrashLoopBackOff state due to repeated container crashes
Finding: ImagePullBackOff due to DNS resolution failure
Finding: Container exit code 1 causing CrashLoopBackOff
recommendations: Verify DNS server configuration at 172.18.0.1 and ensure it's properly resolving registry-1.docker.io
Check kubelet DNS settings (--cluster-dns flag) and ensure they point to a healthy DNS server
Test DNS resolution from the node using nslookup/dig for registry-1.docker.io
Restart kubelet service if DNS configuration changes are made

question: Why is crash-demo in CrashLoopBackOff?
root_cause: DNS resolution failure for Docker registry due to misconfigured/failing DNS server at 172.18.0.1:53
findings: kubernetes: DNS resolution failure for registry-1.docker.io due to misconfigured or failing DNS server at 173.18.0.1:53
loki: Container repeatedly crashing immediately after startup, triggering CrashLoopBackOff due to failed container health checks
Finding: ImagePullBackOff due to DNS resolution failure
Finding: CrashLoopBackOff from failed container startup
Finding: Repeated failed image pulls with DNS lookup errors
recommendations: Fix/replace failing DNS server at 172.18.0.1:53 with reliable DNS (e.g. 8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1)
Verify kubelet configuration for correct DNS settings
Test DNS resolution for registry-1.docker.io manually using nslookup/dig
Check network policies/firewalls blocking DNS traffic to 172.18.0.1:53

question: Why is trace-demo returning HTTP 500?
root_cause: Potential misconfigured Prometheus resource limits or scrape targets leading to underutilized CPU
findings: prometheus: CPU usage is extremely low (0.55%) indicating potential metric collection issues or underutilized resources
recommendations: Verify Prometheus resource limits and requests in Kubernetes deployment
Check scrape configuration for optimal interval and target efficiency
Monitor metric volume and adjust Prometheus scaling if necessary
